title: ssize_t to int cast truncation in generate_eui64 log
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:111,133

hwid_len is ssize_t but is cast to int for logging:
- Line 111: LOG_ERR("hwinfo returned invalid length: %d", (int)hwid_len)
- Line 133: LOG_DBG("EUI-64 from hashed hardware ID (%d bytes)", (int)hwid_len)

What goes wrong: ssize_t can be 64-bit on some platforms while int is 32-bit. Values > INT_MAX would wrap, displaying incorrect values in logs. The actual hwid_len is bounded by sizeof(hwid)=32, so no actual bug here, but the pattern is incorrect.

Fix: Use %zd format specifier with (ssize_t)hwid_len or cast to (long) with %ld for maximum portability.
close_reason: Added comment explaining ssize_t to int cast is safe since hwinfo_get_device_id returns at most 32 bytes
